description: Create packages/edge-config/ - Edge/CDN optimization package with reusable Next.js middleware (geolocation routing, A/B testing, bot detection, rate limiting at edge), CDN cache headers, and static optimization configs. Then update all Next.js apps with optimized next.config (image optimization AVIF/WebP, font optimization, security headers including CSP).
steps: Create packages/edge-config/package.json with name @quant/edge-config, deps: zod, @quant/common. Scripts: build/typecheck/test/lint
Create packages/edge-config/src/types.ts - EdgeConfig, CachePolicy, GeoRouteRule, ABTestConfig, BotPattern types
Create packages/edge-config/src/cdn-headers.ts - CacheControlPolicies class with per-content-type policies (static assets long cache, API no-cache, HTML short cache with stale-while-revalidate)

Create packages/edge-config/src/edge-middleware.ts - createEdgeMiddleware() factory function that composes geolocation routing, A/B testing (using cookie + feature flag integration), bot detection (via User-Agent patterns), edge rate limiting (sliding window via headers)
Create packages/edge-config/src/static-optimization.ts - getImageOptimizationConfig(), getFontOptimizationConfig(), getBundleAnalyzerConfig() returning Next.js config objects
Create packages/edge-config/src/security-headers.ts - getSecurityHeaders() returning CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy
acceptance_criteria: pnpm turbo test --filter=@quant/edge-config passes with all tests green
findings: All checks pass cleanly: typecheck, build, 27 tests (3 test files), lint. Pre-commit hook requires NVM sourced for pnpm path. noUncheckedIndexedAccess in root tsconfig requires guarding array index accesses in tests.
